audit_basis: Phase 3 verification against the live authoritative Cardcom V11 OpenAPI spec (https://secure.cardcom.solutions/swagger/v11/swagger.json, self-reported version 11.0), parsed directly for endpoint paths, schema property names, and enum values; plus WebSearch/WebFetch on Israeli VAT sources and npm/GitHub for community libraries. Removed a fabricated Documents/CreateTaxInvoice endpoint that does not exist in the spec.

claims.claim: Cardcom V11 is the current REST API as of 2026; no public V12 exists.
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom V11 is the current REST API as of 2026; no public V12 exists.
claims.where_in_skill: SKILL.md Overview, references/api-endpoints.md API Version section

claims.claim: Production base URL is https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/
claims.raw_snippet: Production base URL is https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/ Support center: https://support.cardcom.solutions . Currency table row: `1` = ILS, `2` = USD. Sample DocumentUrl in the validator fixture: https://secure.cardcom.solutions/doc/10042 (DocumentNumber 10042, illustrative).

claims.claim: Every V11 endpoint returns ResponseCode (int) and Description (string); ResponseCode == 0 means success.
claims.raw_snippet: Every V11 endpoint returns ResponseCode (int) and Description (string); ResponseCode == 0 means success.

claims.claim: Cardcom supports Bit, PayPal, Apple Pay, and Google Pay as alternative payment methods alongside credit cards.
claims.source_url: https://www.cardcom.solutions/ (homepage 'one-stop-shop', 'respects all credit brands including Bit and digital wallets')
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom supports Bit, PayPal, Apple Pay, and Google Pay as alternative payment methods alongside credit cards.
claims.where_in_skill: SKILL.md Alternative Payment Methods table, Example 5

claims.claim: Bit payments are returned as a separate UrlToBit field on CreateLowProfileResponse.
claims.raw_snippet: Bit payments are returned as a separate UrlToBit field on CreateLowProfileResponse.

claims.claim: PayPal is returned as a separate UrlToPayPal field on CreateLowProfileResponse.
claims.raw_snippet: PayPal is returned as a separate UrlToPayPal field on CreateLowProfileResponse.

claims.claim: Apple Pay and Google Pay are rendered as wallet buttons inside the hosted Low Profile page itself once enabled on the terminal; no separate URL field exists in the API response.
claims.source_url: Inference: cardcom.solutions confirms wallet support; CreateLowProfileResponse schema in V11 docs exposes only UrlToBit and UrlToPayPal as alternative-method URL fields.
claims.raw_snippet: Apple Pay and Google Pay are rendered as wallet buttons inside the hosted Low Profile page itself once enabled on the terminal; no separate URL field exists in the API response.
claims.where_in_skill: SKILL.md Alternative Payment Methods table, Gotchas

claims.claim: DocumentTypeToCreate is a string enum (e.g. 'TaxInvoiceAndReceipt'), not an integer code. Integer codes belong to legacy .aspx interfaces.
claims.raw_snippet: DocumentTypeToCreate is a string enum (e.g. 'TaxInvoiceAndReceipt'), not an integer code. Integer codes belong to legacy .aspx interfaces.

claims.claim: V11 Document object field spellings: Languge (missing second 'a', on the Document and DocumentTran schemas), ISOCoinID/ISOCoinId, IsSendByEmail, TaxId. The DocumentLP sub-schema (LowProfile flow) uses the correctly-spelled Language, and the LowProfile/Create request carries a top-level Language field.
claims.raw_snippet: V11 Document object field spellings: Languge (missing second 'a', on the Document and DocumentTran schemas), ISOCoinID/ISOCoinId, IsSendByEmail, TaxId. The DocumentLP sub-schema (LowProfile flow) uses the correctly-spelled Language, and the LowProfile/Create request carries a top-level Language field.

claims.claim: ApiPassword is required for Documents/CreateDocument and Transactions/RefundByTransactionId, but not for normal LowProfile/Create or Transaction charges.
claims.raw_snippet: ApiPassword is required for Documents/CreateDocument and Transactions/RefundByTransactionId, but not for normal LowProfile/Create or Transaction charges.

claims.claim: Israeli VAT rate is 18% as of 2026 (raised from 17% in January 2025; the 2026 budget proposal to raise to 19% was rejected).
claims.raw_snippet: Israeli VAT rate is 18% as of 2026 (raised from 17% in January 2025; the 2026 budget proposal to raise to 19% was rejected).

claims.claim: PCI DSS v4.0 became mandatory March 31, 2025; all future-dated requirements moved from best-practice to mandatory at that date.
claims.raw_snippet: PCI DSS v4.0 became mandatory March 31, 2025; all future-dated requirements moved from best-practice to mandatory at that date.

claims.claim: Hosted Low Profile keeps merchants in SAQ-A scope; direct server-to-server Transaction with raw CardNumber/CVV2 falls under SAQ-D.
claims.raw_snippet: Hosted Low Profile keeps merchants in SAQ-A scope; direct server-to-server Transaction with raw CardNumber/CVV2 falls under SAQ-D.

claims.claim: Cardcom supports two recurring-payment flavours: card-based (charge a stored Token) and MASAV bank standing orders (direct bank debit via RecuringPayments endpoints).
claims.source_url: https://www.cardcom.solutions/recurring-payments ('Credit card charges' + 'Bank standing orders via MASAV')
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom supports two recurring-payment flavours: card-based (charge a stored Token) and MASAV bank standing orders (direct bank debit via RecuringPayments endpoints).

claims.claim: Cardcom settlement timing is configured per terminal: weekly settlement deposits on the Wednesday following the transaction; monthly settlement deposits on the 6th of the following month.
claims.source_url: Cardcom pricing/fees descriptions surfaced via Israeli comparison pages and Cardcom's own pricing collateral (1st-to-second-to-last credited on 6th of following month)
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom settlement timing is configured per terminal: weekly settlement deposits on the Wednesday following the transaction; monthly settlement deposits on the 6th of the following month.

claims.claim: Cardcom 2026 pricing is roughly 1.2%-1.4% per transaction, with optional monthly plans around 59 NIS/month for the invoicing add-on.
claims.source_url: Multiple Israeli comparison pages and Cardcom's pricing page (https://www.cardcom.solutions/עמלת-סליקה-לעסקים/)
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom 2026 pricing is roughly 1.2%-1.4% per transaction, with optional monthly plans around 59 NIS/month for the invoicing add-on. Hebrew mirror: כ-59 ש"ח לחודש. Illustrative example prompts use amounts like 149 NIS / 149 שח monthly (sample figures, not a pricing claim).

claims.claim: Cardcom's main competitors as Israeli payment gateways are Tranzila, Israpay, and Bit Business.
claims.source_url: https://nowpayments.io/blog/payment-gateway-israel and other 2026 Israeli payment-gateway comparison pages
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom's main competitors as Israeli payment gateways are Tranzila, Israpay, and Bit Business.

claims.claim: RecuringPayments path uses single 'r' spelling in the V11 schema (RecuringPayments, not RecurringPayments).
claims.raw_snippet: RecuringPayments path uses single 'r' spelling in the V11 schema (RecuringPayments, not RecurringPayments).

claims.claim: Test terminal 1000 with the demo ApiName allows API testing without real charges; test card 4580000000000000 with any future expiry and CVV 123.
claims.source_url: https://secure.cardcom.solutions/Api/v11/Docs ; @tsdiapi/cardcom and other community libraries that document the same test creds
claims.raw_snippet: Test terminal 1000 with the demo ApiName allows API testing without real charges; test card 4580000000000000 with any future expiry and CVV 123.

claims.claim: Operation enum values on LowProfile/Create: ChargeOnly (default), ChargeAndCreateToken, CreateTokenOnly, SuspendedDeal, Do3DSAndSubmit.
claims.raw_snippet: Operation enum values on LowProfile/Create: ChargeOnly (default), ChargeAndCreateToken, CreateTokenOnly, SuspendedDeal, Do3DSAndSubmit.

claims.claim: Cardcom's standout differentiator versus other Israeli gateways is automatic generation of Israeli tax documents (חשבונית מס + קבלה) bundled into the payment flow.
claims.raw_snippet: Cardcom's standout differentiator versus other Israeli gateways is automatic generation of Israeli tax documents (חשבונית מס + קבלה) bundled into the payment flow.

claims.claim: Transaction endpoint J2/J5 validation-only operations return ResponseCode 700 or 701 which also count as success.
claims.raw_snippet: Transaction endpoint J2/J5 validation-only operations return ResponseCode 700 or 701 which also count as success.

claims.claim: @tsdiapi/cardcom is an active community-maintained TypeScript/Node.js client for V11 covering payments, refunds, tokenization, and transaction queries.
claims.raw_snippet: @tsdiapi/cardcom is an active community-maintained TypeScript/Node.js client for V11 covering payments, refunds, tokenization, and transaction queries. CardCom also publishes official OpenFields example repos: https://github.com/CardCom/OpenFields-FrontEnd-React and https://github.com/CardCom/OpenFields-Backend-Node (both live, HTTP 200).

claims.claim: Suspended deals (Operation SuspendedDeal) return a SuspendedDealId and are charged later via the SuspendedDeals endpoint group, which includes SuspendedDeals/Charge, SuspendedDeals/Cancel, and SuspendedDeals/GetSuspendedDealInfo.
claims.raw_snippet: Suspended deals (Operation SuspendedDeal) return a SuspendedDealId and are charged later via the SuspendedDeals endpoint group, which includes SuspendedDeals/Charge, SuspendedDeals/Cancel, and SuspendedDeals/GetSuspendedDealInfo.

claims.claim: The production endpoint URLs used in the examples all exist in the live V11 OpenAPI spec: https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/LowProfile/Create, https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/LowProfile/GetLpResult, https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/Documents/CreateDocument, https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/Transactions/Transaction, https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/Transactions/RefundByTransactionId.
claims.raw_snippet: https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/LowProfile/Create https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/LowProfile/GetLpResult https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/Documents/CreateDocument https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/Transactions/Transaction https://secure.cardcom.solutions/api/v11/Transactions/RefundByTransactionId

claims.claim: Redirect/webhook URLs in the code samples are illustrative placeholders on example.com, not live endpoints.
claims.raw_snippet: https://example.com/success https://example.com/failed https://example.com/webhook
